operating_state: Total revenue declined 9% to $21.30 billion driven by automotive sales decreasing 13% from lower average selling prices and reduced deliveries due to updated Model 3 production ramp at Fremont and factory shutdowns at Berlin from Red Sea shipping disruptions and arson attack. Cybertruck deliveries increased as production ramped while services revenue increased from higher Supercharging, insurance, maintenance and used vehicle sales.
cost_structure: Total automotive gross margin compressed from 21.1% to 18.5% primarily from price reductions and temporary manufacturing under-utilization during Cybertruck and updated Model 3 ramps, partially offset by lower raw material costs and freight expenses. Energy storage gross margin expanded dramatically from 11.0% to 24.6% driven by higher Megapack pricing and increased IRA manufacturing credits recognition. Research and development expenses surged from AI investments and proprietary battery cell development.
material_events: Capital expenditures increased to $2.77 billion from $2.07 billion for global factory expansion, machinery and AI-related investments. Net debt proceeds increased while debt repayments rose. Operating cash flow declined significantly to $242 million from $2.51 billion due to unfavorable working capital changes and lower net income.
macro_exposures: Cyclical automotive industry sensitivity to political uncertainty, trade regulations and inflationary pressures impacting vehicle affordability through higher interest rates affecting lease and finance arrangements. Foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations on significant intercompany balances created favorable variance as company does not typically hedge currency risk. Supply chain disruptions from Red Sea conflict and geopolitical tensions affecting production scheduling.
forward_outlook: Capital expenditures expected to exceed $10.00 billion in 2024 for manufacturing capacity expansion, next generation platform development and autonomy investments. Management focused on profitable growth through existing factory leverage, cost reduction, vertical integration and FSD capability deployment including planned robotaxi product. Energy storage production ramping with new Shanghai Megafactory construction and continued Lathrop facility expansion.
uncertainty_profile: Manufacturing ramp uncertainties for new products and technologies across concurrent international projects subject to component constraints and labor shortages. Automotive demand cyclicality and competitive pressures requiring continued execution. Energy storage deployment timing varies meaningfully quarter-to-quarter based on project milestones while maintaining adequate battery cell supply across automotive and energy products.
